([Latin](/wiki/Latin_language "Latin language"): *Societas Verbi Divini*), abbreviated SVD and popularly called the **Verbites** or the **Divine Word Missionaries**, and sometimes the **Steyler Missionaries**, is a [Catholic](/wiki/Catholic_Church "Catholic Church") clerical [religious congregation](/wiki/Religious_congregation "Religious congregation") of [Pontifical Right](/wiki/Pontifical_Right "Pontifical Right") for men. As of 2020, it consisted of 5,965 members composed of [priests](/wiki/Priesthood_in_the_Catholic_Church "Priesthood in the Catholic Church") and [religious brothers](/wiki/Religious_brother "Religious brother") working in more than 70 countries, now part of VIVAT international. It is one of the largest [missionary congregations](/wiki/Missionary_religious_institutes_and_societies "Missionary religious institutes and societies") in the Catholic Church.[[6]](#cite_note-6) Its members add the nominal letters **SVD** after their names to indicate membership in the Congregation. The [superior general](/wiki/Superior_general "Superior general") is Anselmo Ricardo Ribeiro, who hails from Brazil.

The **Society of the Divine Word** ([Latin](/wiki/Latin_language "Latin language"): *Societas Verbi Divini*), abbreviated SVD and popularly called the **Verbites** or the **Divine Word Missionaries**, and sometimes the **Steyler Missionaries**, is a [Catholic](/wiki/Catholic_Church "Catholic Church") clerical [religious congregation](/wiki/Religious_congregation "Religious congregation") of [Pontifical Right](/wiki/Pontifical_Right "Pontifical Right") for men. As of 2020, it consisted of 5,965 members composed of [priests](/wiki/Priesthood_in_the_Catholic_Church "Priesthood in the Catholic Church") and [religious brothers](/wiki/Religious_brother "Religious brother") working in more than 70 countries, now part of VIVAT international. It is one of the largest [missionary congregations](/wiki/Missionary_religious_institutes_and_societies "Missionary religious institutes and societies") in the Catholic Church.[[6]](#cite_note-6) Its members add the nominal letters **SVD** after their names to indicate membership in the Congregation. The [superior general](/wiki/Superior_general "Superior general") is Anselmo Ricardo Ribeiro, who hails from Brazil.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Society_of_the_Divine_Word&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Main article: [Arnold Janssen](/wiki/Arnold_Janssen "Arnold Janssen")

The Society was founded in [Steyl](/wiki/Steyl "Steyl") in the [Netherlands](/wiki/Netherlands "Netherlands") in 1875 by [Arnold Janssen](/wiki/Arnold_Janssen "Arnold Janssen"), a [diocesan priest](/wiki/Diocesan_priest "Diocesan priest"), and drawn mostly from [German](/wiki/German_people "German people") priests and religious exiles in the Netherlands during the church-state conflict called the *[Kulturkampf](/wiki/Kulturkampf "Kulturkampf")*, which had resulted in many religious groups being expelled and  [seminaries](/wiki/Seminary "Seminary") being closed in Germany.[[7]](#cite_note-Limbrock-7)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d0/Saint_Arnold_Janssen%2C_SVD.jpg/250px-Saint_Arnold_Janssen%2C_SVD.jpg)](/wiki/File:Saint_Arnold_Janssen,_SVD.jpg)

[Arnold Janssen](/wiki/Arnold_Janssen "Arnold Janssen") (1837-1909), founder of the Society of the Divine Word

In 1882, the Society started sending missionaries into [China’s](/wiki/China "China") [Shandong](/wiki/Shandong "Shandong") Province, where their aggressive methods were part of the chain of events that led to the [Boxer Uprising](/wiki/Boxer_Uprising "Boxer Uprising") in the late 1890s.[[8]](#cite_note-8) In 1892, missionaries were sent to [Togo](/wiki/Togo "Togo"), a small country in west [Africa](/wiki/Africa "Africa"). The Togo mission was particularly fruitful, for 15 years later the [Holy See](/wiki/Holy_See "Holy See") had appointed an [Apostolic prefect](/wiki/Apostolic_prefect "Apostolic prefect"). The Society’s third mission was to [German New Guinea](/wiki/German_New_Guinea "German New Guinea"), (the northern half of present-day [Papua New Guinea](/wiki/Papua_New_Guinea "Papua New Guinea")).[[7]](#cite_note-Limbrock-7) A mission was also opened in [Paraguay](/wiki/Paraguay "Paraguay").[[9]](#cite_note-9)

In the 20th century the Society further expanded, opening communities in [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia"),[[10]](#cite_note-10) [Botswana](/wiki/Botswana "Botswana") ([Gaborone](/wiki/Gaborone "Gaborone"), [Gumare](/wiki/Gumare "Gumare") and [Ghanzi](/wiki/Ghanzi "Ghanzi")); [Brazil](/wiki/Brazil "Brazil"); [Canada](/wiki/Canada "Canada") ([Quebec](/wiki/Quebec "Quebec") and [Ontario](/wiki/Ontario "Ontario")); [Indonesia](/wiki/Indonesia "Indonesia"); [South Africa](/wiki/South_Africa "South Africa") ([Phalaborwa](/wiki/Phalaborwa "Phalaborwa"), [Polokwane](/wiki/Polokwane "Polokwane") and [Pretoria](/wiki/Pretoria "Pretoria")); the [United States of America](/wiki/United_States_of_America "United States of America") ([Appalachia](/wiki/Appalachia "Appalachia") and [Illinois](/wiki/Illinois "Illinois")); and [Zambia](/wiki/Zambia "Zambia") ([Kabwe](/wiki/Kabwe "Kabwe"), [Livingstone](/wiki/Livingstone,_Zambia "Livingstone, Zambia"), and [Lusaka](/wiki/Lusaka "Lusaka")). Additional European communities were established in [Austria](/wiki/Austria "Austria") ([Bischofshofen](/wiki/Bischofshofen "Bischofshofen") near [Salzburg](/wiki/Salzburg "Salzburg") and [Vienna](/wiki/Vienna "Vienna")); the [Netherlands](/wiki/Netherlands "Netherlands") ([Tegelen](/wiki/Tegelen "Tegelen")); [Rome](/wiki/Rome "Rome"); the [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom") and in the [Silesian](/wiki/Silesia "Silesia") area in [Poland](/wiki/Poland "Poland") (where Mirosław Piątkowski invented in 1994 a new [devotion](/wiki/Catholic_devotions "Catholic devotions"), the [Chaplet of the Holy Spirit and His Seven Gifts](/wiki/Chaplet_of_the_Holy_Spirit_and_His_Seven_Gifts "Chaplet of the Holy Spirit and His Seven Gifts")).[[11]](#cite_note-:1-11)

## Present day

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Society_of_the_Divine_Word&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Present day")]

Many religious orders and congregations have certain characteristics or traits that make them known. The Divine Word Missionaries are recognised by what are called the four characteristic dimensions: the [Bible](/wiki/Bible "Bible"), Mission Animation, Communication, Justice and Peace and Integrity of Creation (JPIC).[[12]](#cite_note-12) With regards to the missions, what makes the SVD unique from many missionary institutes is that mission areas or regions are not the sole responsibility of individual provinces, but of the whole Society. The SVD generalate may appoint members from any country to any other country with priority given to those places which are most in need. This also explains why many SVD communities are international in character.

The SVD has two sister congregations, also founded by Saint Arnold Janssen. They are the [Missionary Sisters Servants of the Holy Spirit](/wiki/Missionary_Sisters_Servants_of_the_Holy_Spirit "Missionary Sisters Servants of the Holy Spirit") (SSpS),[[7]](#cite_note-Limbrock-7) otherwise known as the "Blue Sisters" and a  [contemplative](/wiki/Contemplative_prayer "Contemplative prayer") branch called the [Sister Servants of the Holy Spirit of Perpetual Adoration](/wiki/Holy_Spirit_Adoration_Sisters "Holy Spirit Adoration Sisters") (SSpSAP) or better known as the "Pink Sisters"; the nicknames allude to the colour of the respective [religious habits](/wiki/Religious_habit "Religious habit").

The order elected the Afro-Brazilian priest Anselmo Ricardo Ribeiro as its superior general in 2024.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

## Vows

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Society_of_the_Divine_Word&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Vows")]

As members of a [religious institute](/wiki/Religious_institute "Religious institute") the Missionaries of the Divine Word embrace the [evangelical counsels](/wiki/Evangelical_counsels "Evangelical counsels"), taking the three traditional [religious vows](/wiki/Religious_vows "Religious vows") of poverty, chastity and obedience. Poverty means that all possessions are held in common and that no member may accumulate wealth. Chastity means more than abstaining from sexual activity and its purpose is to make the religious totally available for service; it is also a sign that only [God](/wiki/God "God") can completely fill the human heart. For a member of a religious congregation, obedience is not slavishly doing what one is told by the superior but being attentive to God’s will by prayerfully listening to the voice of the person in charge. Ultimately, these vows are lived out within a community and bolstered by a relationship with God.

The core formula of the solemn Vows of the Divine Word Missionaries, as cited in their constitutions, is as follows:

> ″Therefore I, (name), solemnly promise you, Father, Son and Holy Spirit, to live for (x) year / for life of chastity, poverty, and obedience, according to the constitutions of the Society of the Divine Word. I make these vows before you, (Provincial Superior), as the representative of the Superior General and in the presence of this community.″

## Religious formation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Society_of_the_Divine_Word&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Religious formation")]

In the initial stages, those interested in joining the congregation have several meetings with an SVD priest, usually with visits to a community. During this time the members of the congregation share what it is like to be a  [priest](/wiki/Presbyter "Presbyter"), or a religious [brother](/wiki/Lay_brother "Lay brother"). Those who are enquiring about entering the congregation are strongly encouraged to attend [Mass](/wiki/Mass_(Liturgy) "Mass (Liturgy)") as often as possible, to read the [Sacred Scriptures](/wiki/Bible "Bible") especially the [Gospel](/wiki/Gospel "Gospel") accounts and to regularly spend time in [prayer](/wiki/Prayer "Prayer") in order to better discern their vocation.

### Pre-novitiate

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Society_of_the_Divine_Word&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Pre-novitiate")]

This is a yearlong experience of living in an SVD community, sharing in many aspects of the life of the congregation. "The goal of the Pre-novitiate is to enable the student to experience religious missionary life in community, deepen his own understanding of vocation and continue the initial learning about the SVD, its charism, its origins, history and mission."[[14]](#cite_note-14) During this time the candidates participate in the prayer life of a community, share more deeply with others and become involved in one of more of the congregation’s apostolates. Essentially, it is an extended period of discernment for the postulants and an opportunity for the congregation to assess the strengths of the candidates and possible areas requiring growth.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e8/Divine_Word_College.jpg/250px-Divine_Word_College.jpg)](/wiki/File:Divine_Word_College.jpg)

[Divine Word College](/wiki/Divine_Word_College "Divine Word College") Seminary in Epworth, Iowa, United States.

### Novitiate

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Society_of_the_Divine_Word&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Novitiate")]

Next follows the [novitiate](/wiki/Novitiate "Novitiate") which is the time for preparing to take the vows of poverty, chastity and obedience. The novitiate year is crucial, for it is then “…that the novices better understand their divine vocation, and indeed one which is proper to the institute, experience the manner of living of the institute, and form their mind and heart in its spirit, and so that their intention and suitability are tested.”[[15]](#cite_note-15)

> ”The Novitiate provides a special time and environment for the nurturing of a growing vocation; it does not give birth to the vocation. The Novitiate builds upon what is already under way in a person's life. It serves to mature and clarify a vocation in accordance with the religious life style and the special charism of the Society.”[[16]](#cite_note-16)

Thus, the novices are given the opportunity for longer periods of [prayer](/wiki/Prayer "Prayer") and spiritual reading as well as silence in order to reflect on the vocation [God](/wiki/God "God") is offering and nature of their response. The spiritual development of the novice is of particular focus, especially through [spiritual direction](/wiki/Spiritual_direction "Spiritual direction"). During the novitiate the history and Constitutions of the Congregation are studied in depth. A simple profession is made at the end of the novitiate and the person officially becomes a member of the Society, for

> “By religious profession, members assume the observance of the three evangelical counsels by public vow, are consecrated to God through the ministry of the Church, and are incorporated into the institute with the rights and duties defined by law.”[[17]](#cite_note-17)

### Post-novitiate

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Society_of_the_Divine_Word&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Post-novitiate")]

After the novitiate, the new members of the congregation continue their studies. For those preparing for [Holy Orders](/wiki/Holy_Orders "Holy Orders") this normally involves a 4-year [theology](/wiki/Theology "Theology") degree. In the [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States") students attend [Catholic Theological Union](/wiki/Catholic_Theological_Union "Catholic Theological Union").[[18]](#cite_note-18) In Australia, studies are taken at the [Melbourne College of Divinity](/wiki/Melbourne_College_of_Divinity "Melbourne College of Divinity") after which students are strongly encouraged to spend a year in a foreign mission before proceeding to [ordination](/wiki/Ordination "Ordination").[[19]](#cite_note-19) Filipino students attend the Society’s own [Divine Word Seminary](/wiki/Divine_Word_Seminary "Divine Word Seminary") in [Tagaytay](/wiki/Tagaytay "Tagaytay"). Final vows are taken immediately before ordination to the [diaconate](/wiki/Diaconate "Diaconate") which is followed by ordination to the priesthood between six months and a year later. In [Indonesia](/wiki/Indonesia "Indonesia") students attend St. Paul Major Seminary-Ledalero.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/37/Divine_Word_Theologate_Chicago%2C_USA.jpg/250px-Divine_Word_Theologate_Chicago%2C_USA.jpg)](/wiki/File:Divine_Word_Theologate_Chicago,_USA.jpg)

The Divine Word Theologate in [Chicago](/wiki/Chicago "Chicago"), [Illinois](/wiki/Illinois "Illinois"), United States.

Those whose [vocation](/wiki/Vocation "Vocation") is to the [brotherhood](/wiki/Lay_brother "Lay brother") pursue studies which are suited to developing their talents and interests. The Society is conscious that some regard brothers as being lower than priests and, in response, it states:

> “Religious Brothers, by their life and ministry play a prophetic role in the Society and in the Church. They remind us all of the common dignity and fundamental Brotherhood of Christians: "You are all Brothers," ([Matthew 23:8.](/wiki/Gospel_of_Saint_Matthew "Gospel of Saint Matthew")) Furthermore, Brothers keep alive the sense of authentic communion in our communities and our [unity in diversity](/wiki/Unity_in_diversity "Unity in diversity"), which is expressed by their being consecrated laymen who live together with clerical confreres. (SVD Constitutions,104)
> Missionary work is not tied to ordination. Hence, we should keep in mind that Brothers make a great and equal contribution to mission through their professional work, social services and pastoral ministry. As non-ordained missionaries, brothers are able reach out to the laity, especially to faith-seekers and people of other religious traditions. Together with ordained confreres they bring fullness to the "Missio Dei" in contemporary world.”[[20]](#cite_note-20)

Vows are renewed annually; after three years a member may request final vows. According to  [Canon law](/wiki/Canon_law_(Catholic_Church) "Canon law (Catholic Church)"), temporary vows may be renewed for a longer period but not exceeding nine years.[[21]](#cite_note-21)

## Britain and Ireland

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Society_of_the_Divine_Word&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Britain and Ireland")]

The Society came to Britain in 1931 and to Ireland in 1939. St. Richards College in Birmingham became its seminary and boarding school. In Ireland [Donamon Castle](/wiki/Donamon_Castle "Donamon Castle"), Co. Roscommon became its noviciate and philosophy school, in 1980 its students moved to [Maynooth College](/wiki/Maynooth_College "Maynooth College"). The Divine Word Missions produced the magazine [The Word (Divine Word Missionaries)](/w/index.php?title=The_Word_(Divine_Word_Missionaries)&action=edit&redlink=1 "The Word (Divine Word Missionaries) (page does not exist)") in Maynooth they set up training and media company [Kairos Communications](/wiki/Kairos_Communications "Kairos Communications")[[22]](#cite_note-22) which produces programmes for TV, and runs the postgraduate course in conjunction with St. Patrick's College, Maynooth[[23]](#cite_note-23) and the media studies degree with [National University of Ireland, Maynooth](/wiki/National_University_of_Ireland,_Maynooth "National University of Ireland, Maynooth"). SVD House provides student accommodation in Maynooth.[[24]](#cite_note-24)

## Botswana

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Society_of_the_Divine_Word&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Botswana")]

In a departure from the traditional sources of income used by many religious congregations which run schools, hospitals and [retreat centres](/wiki/Retreat_(spiritual) "Retreat (spiritual)"), the Divine Word Missionaries who are citizens of Botswana, in collaboration with professional [lay people](/wiki/Laity "Laity"), run "Catholic Safaris".[[25]](#cite_note-25) The idea was to run a safari with a Catholic perspective and aims to support the local ventures, creating also job opportunities and income for the families. The centre serves the Catholic mission territory of the northern and western parts of Botswana.

The members of the province also work with those affected by [HIV](/wiki/HIV "HIV") and [AIDS](/wiki/AIDS "AIDS"), [orphaned](/wiki/Orphan "Orphan") children, refugees, health education, [catechetics](/wiki/Catechism "Catechism"), [Scripture](/wiki/Bible "Bible") study, environmental issues and unemployed young people. They have an outreach mission in Zimbabwe. Their preferred partners in dialogue are:

> "...people who have no faith community and “faith-seekers”
>
> * people who are poor and marginalized
> * people of different cultures
> * people of different religious traditions and secular ideologies"[[26]](#cite_note-26)

## Japan

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Society_of_the_Divine_Word&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Japan")]

The Society operates [Nanzan University](/wiki/Nanzan_University "Nanzan University") in [Nagoya](/wiki/Nagoya "Nagoya"), Japan. In 1932 the German SVD missionary Joseph Reiners (1874-1945) founded Nanzan High School in Nagoya. In 1946, the Nanzan College of Foreign Studies was established, which comprised an English Department and Chinese Department, with German and French Departments added in the following year. Nanzan University was formed in 1949.　The Faculty of Literature comprised English, French, German and Chinese Departments.[[27]](#cite_note-27) In addition to the schools run by The Society in Japan, many priests serve as pastors in local Churches in Nagoya, Nagasaki, Akita and Niigata Prefectures, and in Tokyo. The current archbishop of Tokyo, Bishop [Tarcisio Isao Kikuchi](/wiki/Tarcisio_Isao_Kikuchi "Tarcisio Isao Kikuchi"), and the bishop-elect of the Diocese of Niigata in Northeast Japan, [Paul Daisuke Narui](/wiki/Paul_Daisuke_Narui "Paul Daisuke Narui"), are members of the Society of the Divine Word.

## The Philippines

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Society_of_the_Divine_Word&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: The Philippines")]

In the [Philippines](/wiki/Philippines "Philippines"), the Divine Word Missionaries arrived in [Bangued, Abra](/wiki/Bangued,_Abra "Bangued, Abra"), in 1909, founding schools in Bangued, [Vigan](/wiki/Vigan "Vigan"), in [Ilocos Sur](/wiki/Ilocos_Sur "Ilocos Sur") and [Laoag](/wiki/Laoag "Laoag") City in [Ilocos Norte](/wiki/Ilocos_Norte "Ilocos Norte"), as well as in other parts of the Philippines. Now there are about 500 [Filipino](/wiki/Philippines "Philippines") SVD priests and brothers and around 150 of them are serving in overseas missions on all continents. In the Philippines, the SVD have three ecclesiastical provinces, namely: the Philippine North (PHN) that comprises missionary works of Pangasinan to Aparri; the Philippine Central (PHC) that covers the National Capital Region, and all the provinces comprising central Luzon, southern Tagalog and the whole Bicol region; and the Philippine South (PHS) whose ministries cover the Visayas and Mindanao Islands.  [Saint Jude Catholic School](/wiki/Saint_Jude_Catholic_School_(Manila) "Saint Jude Catholic School (Manila)"), a school in [Manila](/wiki/Manila "Manila") near [Malacañan Palace](/wiki/Malaca%C3%B1an_Palace "Malacañan Palace"), is an SVD school. The congregation opened Christ the King Mission Seminary in 1934 in [Quezon City](/wiki/Quezon_City "Quezon City") for their Filipino seminarians and from then on their numbers continued to increase eventually making the SVD the largest [religious institute](/wiki/Religious_institute "Religious institute") of men in the country.

### Philippine Southern Province

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Society_of_the_Divine_Word&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Philippine Southern Province")]

The SVD Philippine Southern Province works on three areas: formation, education and pastoral ministries. In formation, young candidates for the priesthood are trained in the Divine Word Formation Center in [Davao City](/wiki/Davao_City "Davao City") while seminarians for brotherhood are formed in the Freinademetz Formation House in [Cebu City](/wiki/Cebu_City "Cebu City"). In education, the SVDs run the [University of San Carlos](/wiki/University_of_San_Carlos "University of San Carlos") in [Cebu City](/wiki/Cebu_City "Cebu City"); [Holy Name University](/wiki/Holy_Name_University "Holy Name University") in [Tagbilaran City](/wiki/Tagbilaran_City "Tagbilaran City"); and the [Liceo del Verbo Divino](/wiki/Liceo_del_Verbo_Divino "Liceo del Verbo Divino") (formerly Divine Word University) of [Tacloban City](/wiki/Tacloban_City "Tacloban City"). In pastoral ministries, the SVDs have one parish in [Cebu City](/wiki/Cebu_City "Cebu City"), five parishes in [Surigao del Norte](/wiki/Surigao_del_Norte "Surigao del Norte"), six in [Agusan del Sur](/wiki/Agusan_del_Sur "Agusan del Sur") and two in Zamboanga, Sibugay, and [Olutanga Island](/wiki/Olutanga_Island "Olutanga Island"). They manage radio stations, the Steyler Canteen, Catholic Trade Cebu, Inc., and a retreat house. They also work in cooperatives, adopted communities, and depressed areas and dialogue with faith seekers and other religions. Their vision statement is as follows:

> "We, members of the Society of the Divine Word (SVD), an international religious-missionary congregation of brothers and priests, founded by Saint Arnold Janssen and named after the Divine Word, envision a world where dialogue is possible because people, inspired by the Word of God and empowered by the Spirit, respect the uniqueness of each person and accept all nations and peoples."[[28]](#cite_note-28)

## United States

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Society_of_the_Divine_Word&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: United States")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4e/SVD_Presence_in_North_America.jpg/250px-SVD_Presence_in_North_America.jpg)](/wiki/File:SVD_Presence_in_North_America.jpg)

A map and detailed list of the parishes and houses of the Divine Word Missionaries in the three Provinces (West, South and Chicago) in North America.

In 1905 the Divine Word Missionaries began work in [African American](/wiki/African_Americans "African Americans") parishes in [Mississippi](/wiki/Mississippi "Mississippi"), and [St Augustine Seminary](/w/index.php?title=St_Augustine_Seminary_(Bay_St._Louis)&action=edit&redlink=1 "St Augustine Seminary (Bay St. Louis) (page does not exist)"), the first school specifically for the training of African American men for the Catholic priesthood, was opened in [Bay St. Louis, Mississippi](/wiki/Bay_St._Louis,_Mississippi "Bay St. Louis, Mississippi") shortly thereafter.

Divine Word Missionary priests have long worked in African American communities in [Louisiana](/wiki/Louisiana "Louisiana"), [Arkansas](/wiki/Arkansas "Arkansas"), and Mississippi, in [Chicago](/wiki/Chicago "Chicago"), [Indianapolis](/wiki/Indianapolis "Indianapolis"), [Los Angeles](/wiki/Los_Angeles "Los Angeles"), and elsewhere. Numerous African Americans have joined the Society of the Divine Word as priests and brothers and have served as foreign missionaries throughout the world.[[29]](#cite_note-29)

The Western USA Province has since converted the St Augustine's seminary into a retreat center.[[30]](#cite_note-30)

## Argentina

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Society_of_the_Divine_Word&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Argentina")]

In [Argentina](/wiki/Argentina "Argentina"), the Missionaries of the Divine Word have been present since 1889 and work in the formation of the clergy, especially in the sending of missionaries 'ad gentes'. Argentina became one of the countries with the greatest Verbita presence in the world.[[31]](#cite_note-31)

Currently, it is divided into three regions:

**East**: encompassing the provinces of [Misiones](/w/index.php?title=Provincia_de_Misiones&action=edit&redlink=1 "Provincia de Misiones (page does not exist)") and [Chaco](/wiki/Provincia_del_Chaco "Provincia del Chaco");

**South**: encompassing the provinces of [Buenos Aires](/wiki/Provincia_de_Buenos_Aires "Provincia de Buenos Aires"), [Mendoza](/w/index.php?title=Provincia_de_Mendoza&action=edit&redlink=1 "Provincia de Mendoza (page does not exist)"), [Neuquén](/w/index.php?title=Provincia_del_Neuqu%C3%A9n&action=edit&redlink=1 "Provincia del Neuquén (page does not exist)"), [Santa Fe](/wiki/Provincia_de_Santa_Fe "Provincia de Santa Fe"), [Entre Ríos](/wiki/Entre_R%C3%ADos_Province "Entre Ríos Province"), and [Córdoba](/wiki/Provincia_de_C%C3%B3rdoba_(Argentina) "Provincia de Córdoba (Argentina)");

**North**: [Jujuy](/wiki/Jujuy "Jujuy").

The distinguished institutions are: Instituto Verbo Divino Pilar (Buenos Aires) Colegio Del Salvador (Jujuy)
Centro Educativo Cristo Rey (Córdoba)
Colegio Guadalupe (Buenos Aires)
Colegio San José (Esperanza – Santa Fe)
Instituto José Manuel Estrada (Rafael Calzada – Buenos Aires).
